This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2022 season. This is the Bulls' fourth consecutive season in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]].

==Season review==
{{Expand section|date=January 2024|with=coverage of season after February|small=no}}

===December 2021===

The Bulls announced three season long loans in the form of prop [[Muizz Mustapha]], hooker [[Corey Johnson (rugby league)|Corey Johnson]] and loose forward [[Jarrod O'Connor]] all from local rivals [[Leeds Rhinos]], it was also revealed that the Bulls will play the Rhinos in a pre-season friendly on 23 January.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/19757863.leeds-trio-join-bulls-loan-part-partnership-deal/|title=Leeds trio to join Bulls on loan as part of partnership deal |work=Telegraph & Argus |date=2 December 2021 |accessdate=4 December 2021}}</ref> Trialist [[Jamie Pye]] left the club and signed a permanent deal with [[Oldham R.L.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/oldham-sign-bradford-trialist-jamie-pye/|title=Oldham sign Bradford trialist Jamie Pye |work=Love Rugby League |date=3 December 2021 |accessdate=4 December 2021}}</ref> [[Super League]] side [[Hull F.C.]] revealed that they would once again partner the Bulls in a dual registration for the upcoming season, whilst also announcing they would face the Bulls in a friendly for [[Danny Brough]]'s testimonial.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1277/hull-fc-become-dual-registration-partners|title=HULL FC BECOME DUAL REGISTRATION PARTNERS |work=BRADFORD BULLS |date=15 December 2021 |accessdate=19 December 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1278/bulls-to-face-aerlie-birds-in-brough-testimonial|title=BULLS TO FACE AERLIE BIRDS IN BROUGH TESTIMONIAL |work=BRADFORD BULLS |date=15 December 2021 |accessdate=19 December 2021}}</ref> The 2022 away kit and the squad numbers were released mid-month, with [[Elliot Kear]] taking the number 1 shirt. Other notable squad numbers were new signings [[Kieran Gill]], [[Ryan Millar (rugby league)|Ryan Miller]] and [[Declan Patton]] took the 4,5 and 6 shirts, whilst [[Anthony Walker (rugby league)|Anthony Walker]] was promoted to number 8.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1281/2022-squad-numbers|title=2022 SQUAD NUMBERS |work=BRADFORD BULLS |date=18 December 2021 |accessdate=19 December 2021}}</ref> The Boxing Day clash against Halifax was postponed due to adverse weather conditions.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1289/halifax-game-postponed|title=HALIFAX GAME POSTPONED |work=BRADFORD BULLS |date=26 December 2021 |accessdate=26 December 2021}}</ref>

===January 2022===

Bradford announced another friendly, this time against [[RFL League 1]] side [[Midlands Hurricanes]] at Odsal on 12 February. As this is during the [[RFL Championship]] season, the Bulls will have a mixture of players not selected for the league game and academy/reserve players.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/1292/hurricanes-forecasted-for-odsal-stadium|title=HURRICANES FORECASTED FOR ODSAL STADIUM |work=BRADFORD BULLS |date=5 January 2022 |accessdate=29
